_doc: Resume canon — copy to data/canon.json (gitignored) and replace every value with your own. Source-of-truth for resume content; drives scripts/resume_compose.py. This example is filled with a deliberately broad FICTIONAL persona (James Bond) to show one varied career tailored into many target roles. All contact details are fictional: phone numbers use Ofcom's 020 7946 0xxx range reserved for fiction, and emails use reserved example domains. PII lives in canon.json, not here.

personal.name: James Bond
personal._doc_email: Composer defaults to email_security for resume contact lines. email_personal is the fallback. Rename these freely — they're just two addresses.

employment._doc: id is the canonical role identifier — it MUST match the role_id frontmatter in data/skill_library/<id>.md.
employment.title: Senior Field-Craft & Firearms Instructor
Regional Director (commercial cover for field operations)
Field Intelligence Officer, 00 Section
employment._doc_dates: Use YYYY-MM. 'present' is the special value for a currently-held role.

references._doc: Optional. Composer omits the References section unless --include-references is passed.
references.name: Miles Messervy
references.title: Director

certifications.name: Advanced & Evasive Driving Certification
certifications._doc_status: active | expired. Only active certs surface in the composed resume.

professional_activities._doc: Clubs, memberships, speaking, awards. Composer emits these in the Activities section.
professional_activities.name: Blades Club, London
professional_activities.description: Private members' club; high-stakes bridge and baccarat
